skill.name: server-management
skill.description: Server management principles and decision-making. Process management, monitoring strategy, and scaling decisions. Teaches thinking, not commands.
skill.summary: Server management principles and decision-making for production operations. Teaches process management, monitoring strategy, and scaling decisions.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged two instances of 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at SKILL.md lines 3 and 166. After manual review, these are FALSE POSITIVES. Line 3 contains only a description string in YAML frontmatter, and line 166 contains only instructional text. The file contains no cryptographic code, no network requests, no file system operations, no environment access, and no external command execution. This is purely educational content teaching server management concepts.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Scanner False Positive - Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at SKILL.md:3 and SKILL.md:166. Upon review, line 3 contains only a YAML description field with no cryptographic content, and line 166 contains only instructional text. No cryptographic algorithms present.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Manual inspection confirms no cryptographic code exists. The scanner likely matched a benign text pattern incorrectly.

content.value_statement: Learn server management thinking and decision-making for production systems. This skill teaches process management, monitoring strategy, and scaling principles rather than memorized commands.
actual_capabilities: Guides process management tool selection (PM2, systemd, Docker, Kubernetes)
Teaches monitoring strategy and key metrics prioritization
Explains log management principles and rotation strategies
Provides scaling decision frameworks for different scenarios
Outlines security principles for server access and updates
limitations: Does not execute any server commands directly
Does not provide specific configuration files
Does not include installation or setup instructions
Focuses on principles rather than implementation code

use_cases.title: DevOps Learning
use_cases.description: New developers learning server operations and production concepts

use_cases.title: Architecture Decision Making
use_cases.description: Engineers making decisions about monitoring and scaling strategies

use_cases.title: System Troubleshooting
use_cases.description: Teams establishing troubleshooting workflows and priorities

prompt_templates.title: Process Management Basics
prompt_templates.prompt: What process manager should I use for a Node.js application in production?

prompt_templates.title: Monitoring Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: What key metrics should I monitor for a web application?
prompt_templates.scenario: Setting up monitoring for a new service

prompt_templates.title: Scaling Decisions
prompt_templates.prompt: When should I scale horizontally versus vertically?

prompt_templates.title: Health Check Design
prompt_templates.prompt: What should a proper health check include?

output_examples.input: What process manager should I use for a Node.js application?
output: For Node.js in production, PM2 is recommended. It provides clustering, zero-downtime reload, and auto-restart on crash. Alternative: systemd for Linux-native integration.

output_examples.input: When should I scale horizontally vs vertically?
output: Vertical scaling (more CPU/RAM) offers quick fixes for single-instance bottlenecks. Horizontal scaling (more instances) provides sustainability and distribution. Choose vertical for immediate needs, horizontal for long-term growth.

output_examples.input: What are the key server security principles?
output: Key principles: SSH keys only (no passwords), firewall with minimal ports, regular security updates, environment variables for secrets, comprehensive audit logging.

best_practices: Start monitoring from day one rather than adding it later
Use structured logging (JSON format) for easier parsing and analysis
Implement auto-restart configurations to handle crashes automatically
Choose health check depth based on load balancer requirements
anti_patterns: Running services as root instead of non-root users
Ignoring logs or skipping log rotation setup
Manual restarts instead of configuring auto-restart
Skipping backups or not having a regular backup schedule

faq.question: Does this skill execute commands on my server?
faq.answer: No. This skill teaches principles and decision-making frameworks. It does not run any commands or modify any files.

faq.question: What tools does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill provides guidance for tools like PM2, systemd, Docker, Kubernetes, Grafana, and others mentioned in the principles.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for beginners?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains concepts in plain language with decision tables. It is designed for learners who want to understand thinking, not memorize commands.

faq.question: Does this skill include configuration examples?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on principles and decision criteria. It does not provide specific configuration files or code snippets.

faq.question: How is this skill different from server documentation?
faq.answer: Documentation tells you what commands to run. This skill teaches why to choose certain approaches and when to use them.

faq.question: Can I use this for troubleshooting production issues?
faq.answer: Yes. The troubleshooting priority section provides a systematic approach: check process, logs, resources, network, then dependencies.
